title: Heroes Get Their Day
story: Every November 11, Americans pause to honor the brave men and women who served in the military. Veterans Day celebrates all soldiers, sailors, airmen, and marines who protected our country. Schools often hold special assemblies where veterans share their stories. Many restaurants offer free meals to veterans as a thank you. Parades march down main streets with flags waving proudly. Families visit cemeteries to place flowers on graves of fallen heroes.
activity_question: What do many restaurants do for veterans on Veterans Day?
follow_up_question: How could you thank a veteran in your community?

title: Singles' Day: Shopping Spree Supreme!
story: November 11 is Singles' Day, the world's biggest online shopping event! Started in China, this holiday celebrates being single with massive sales and deals. People stay up until midnight to snag incredible bargains on everything from clothes to electronics. Shopping websites crash from millions of people clicking "buy now" at the same time. Friends throw parties, buy gifts for themselves, and treat it like their personal Christmas in November. Some people spend more money on Singles' Day than during entire holiday seasons!
activity_question: What do people do at midnight on Singles' Day?
bonus_fact: The date 11/11 was chosen because the four "1s" represent single people!

title: Sundae Funday: Ice Cream Gets Fancy
story: Every November 11, ice cream lovers across America celebrate National Sundae Day by creating towering masterpieces of frozen delight! People visit ice cream shops, diners, and soda fountains to build their perfect sundae with scoops of vanilla, chocolate, or strawberry ice cream. The real fun comes with the toppings: hot fudge, caramel sauce, whipped cream, maraschino cherries, crushed nuts, and colorful sprinkles. Families gather around kitchen tables to make DIY sundae bars, letting everyone customize their own sweet creation. Social media fills with photos of elaborate sundae towers that look almost too pretty to eat. Some brave souls even attempt breakfast sundaes with granola and fruit!
activity_question: What do people commonly put on top of their sundaes besides ice cream?
bonus_fact: The ice cream sundae was invented because some places banned ice cream sodas on Sundays!

title: National Metal Day Rocks Hard!
story: Headbangers and music lovers crank up their amplifiers extra loud on November 11 for National Metal Day! This holiday celebrates the thunderous world of heavy metal music with its powerful guitar riffs, pounding drums, and epic vocals. Fans wear their favorite band t-shirts, share metal songs on social media, and attend concerts featuring everything from classic rock to death metal. Music stores often host special events and guitar demonstrations. Some people learn to play their first metal song on this day, while others simply blast their stereo and practice their best air guitar moves in their bedroom!
activity_question: What do fans typically wear to celebrate National Metal Day?
follow_up_question: What metal song would you want to learn to play first?

title: Fold Your Way to Fun Day
story: Paper transforms into magical creatures every November 11 on National Origami Day! People around the world celebrate this ancient Japanese art by folding colorful squares into cranes, flowers, dragons, and even tiny dinosaurs. Schools host origami workshops where kids learn to make jumping frogs and flapping birds. Libraries set up folding stations with instruction books and rainbow paper. Social media fills with photos of incredible creations, from simple butterflies to complex roses. The best part? You only need paper and your hands to join the fun. Many people challenge themselves to learn one new fold each year on this special day.
activity_question: What do people need to participate in National Origami Day?
bonus_fact: The word 'origami' comes from Japanese words meaning 'folding paper.'

title: Stick It to Hunger with Pocky!
story: Crunchy biscuit sticks covered in chocolate, strawberry, or dozens of other flavors take center stage every November 11! National Pocky Day celebrates these popular Japanese snacks that look like skinny pretzel sticks dipped in sweet coatings. People share photos of themselves holding Pocky sticks, try new flavors, and even use them as edible chopsticks. The date was chosen because the number 11 looks just like four Pocky sticks lined up together! Fans post creative Pocky art and challenge friends to eat them without using their hands.
activity_question: Why was November 11 chosen as National Pocky Day?
activity_choices: Because the number 11 looks like four Pocky sticks
Because Pocky was invented on that date
Because it's the start of winter snack season
Because Japanese people love the number 11
activity_answer: Because the number 11 looks like four Pocky sticks
bonus_fact: Pocky comes in over 50 different flavors worldwide, including green tea and purple sweet potato!
